items.name: Fresh King Coconut

treatments.name: Oceanside Beach Massage
treatments.description: Relaxation massage performed in a private cabana on the beach, with the sound of the waves as your soundtrack.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Swedish Relaxation Massage

treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 6 hours in advance.

classes.description: Morning yoga on the beach as the sun rises over the Indian Ocean. All levels welcome.

packages.description: Oceanside massage, sunrise yoga, and a spa lunch by the beach. The perfect escape.

options.name: Colombo Day Trip

experiences.name: Bentota River Sunset Cruise
experiences.description: Glide along the serene Bentota River at golden hour, passing mangroves, cinnamon islands, and local fishermen.

experiences.name: Water Sports Adventure
experiences.description: A thrilling half-day of jet skiing, windsurfing, banana boat rides, and stand-up paddleboarding.

experiences.name: Sea Turtle Watching
experiences.description: Join a guided evening walk to witness Olive Ridley sea turtles nesting on Bentota beach — a truly magical experience.

localPlaces.name: Brief Garden (Bevis Bawa)
localPlaces.description: The remarkable 2-hectare garden estate of artist and landscape architect Bevis Bawa, elder brother of Geoffrey Bawa, set near Kalawila village. Created over 40 years, the garden is a theatrical labyrinth of sculpture, tropical planting, and surprise vistas that reportedly inspired Geoffrey Bawa to create Lunuganga. The house remains furnished as Bevis left it.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Combine with Lunuganga for a full Bawa brothers day. Go in the morning when the light is best for photography.

localPlaces.name: Lunuganga Country Estate (Geoffrey Bawa)
localPlaces.description: Geoffrey Bawa — Asia's most influential architect of the 20th century — purchased this former rubber estate in 1948 and spent the rest of his life reshaping it into a living masterpiece. Guided tours wind through terraced gardens, pavilions, and the beautifully preserved house above Dedduwa Lake. A pilgrimage for anyone with an interest in architecture, landscape, or design.
localPlaces.entryFee: USD 20 (foreign adults) — book directly with the Geoffrey Bawa Trust
localPlaces.hotelNote: Numbers are strictly limited per tour. Our concierge can pre-book on your behalf. Do not miss the Cinnamon Hill viewpoint.

localPlaces.name: Bentota River & Madu Ganga Boat Safari
localPlaces.description: The Bentota River estuary and Madu Ganga wetland (a UNESCO Ramsar site, 915 hectares) are best explored by local boat. Weave through 64 islands, visit a cinnamon-processing island, enjoy a fish-foot spa in the shallows, and spot water monitor lizards, kingfishers, and occasional crocodiles in the mangrove channels.
localPlaces.travelTime: 5 min by tuk-tuk to the jetty
localPlaces.entryFee: USD 25–35 per person for a 2-hour safari (Buddhist temple and fish spa extra)
localPlaces.hotelNote: Book through our boat concierge for a private vessel and skip the larger tour groups. Evening departures catch the best bird activity.

localPlaces.name: Kosgoda Sea Turtle Conservation Project
localPlaces.description: One of Sri Lanka's most respected sea turtle conservation centres, located where five endangered species — Green, Loggerhead, Olive Ridley, Leatherback, and Hawksbill turtles — come ashore to nest. The hatchery rescues eggs, incubates them safely, and releases hatchlings. Visitors can see turtles at all life stages and, for a small extra fee, release a hatchling into the sea.
localPlaces.travelTime: 20 min by tuk-tuk (north along A2 highway)
localPlaces.entryFee: LKR 1,000 foreigners; turtle release LKR 1,500 extra
localPlaces.hotelNote: Evening visits (after 18:00) can coincide with nesting females coming ashore — ask our concierge to arrange a guided night visit during nesting season (November–April).

localPlaces.name: Hikkaduwa Coral Reef & Marine Sanctuary
localPlaces.description: Hikkaduwa's protected coral reef park, just 200 m offshore, is one of the best accessible snorkel and dive sites on Sri Lanka's south-west coast. The shallow reef (1–5 m) teems with parrotfish, surgeonfish, and resident sea turtles. Glass-bottom boat tours are also available for non-swimmers.
localPlaces.entryFee: Snorkel gear rental from LKR 500; glass-bottom boat approx. USD 10
localPlaces.hotelNote: The scenic coastal train from Bentota station to Hikkaduwa takes about 35 minutes and is an experience in itself — our concierge can check the train schedule.

localPlaces.name: Bentota Water Sports Centre
localPlaces.description: The Bentota stretch of beach in front of the resort is Sri Lanka's premier water sports hub, offering jet skiing, windsurfing, banana boat rides, water-skiing, kite surfing, and stand-up paddleboarding in calm, patrolled waters. Experienced international and local instructors are on hand.
localPlaces.hotelNote: The Bentota lagoon (sheltered from the open sea by a sandbar) is ideal for beginners. Best conditions are 09:00–14:00.
